---
title: "RLMHS01: Byrne horseshoe ranking of wave-1 predictors of word-reading gain"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMHS01 is the Byrne analogue of `lrp-rli-hs-001` (#338 Phase D): one
regularised-horseshoe regression of wave-3 BAS word reading on its own wave-1
baseline plus the standardised wave-1 predictor set (`bpvs`, `trog`, `basdig`,
`bassim`, `basnum`, age) under a shared shrinkage prior, ranked by posterior
$P(|\beta| > 0.1)$. It is the ranking cross-check partner of `lrp-rlm-adj-001`
over the **identical frame** (pooled three-group, n = 69, group-nuisance
dummies outside the horseshoe): where the adjusted model reports every slope
at face value, the horseshoe asks which predictors escape aggressive shrinkage.

::: {.callout-important title="A ranking, not effects — and nothing causal"}

The Byrne cohort has no intervention (`causal_status="none"`): this is a
which-predictors-carry-signal read, not an effect estimate. Shrunk
coefficients are not evidence of no association — at n = 69 with a correlated
predictor cluster, the horseshoe deliberately zeroes what it cannot
distinguish from noise.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="No gradient-boosting comparison"}

The RLI horseshoe models cross-check a gradient-boosting ranking; no GB layer
exists for the Byrne cohort, so no `horseshoe_vs_gb` table is produced. The
comparison partner here is the mutually-adjusted model `lrp-rlm-adj-001`.

:::

## Model

As `lrp-rlm-adj-001`'s frame, with the per-predictor slopes drawn from a
regularised ("Finnish") horseshoe (global scale $\tau_0 = 0.1$, slab scale 2,
slab df 4; Piironen & Vehtari 2017) instead of independent
$\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ priors. The group-nuisance dummies stay unshrunk
($\mathrm{Normal}(0,1)$): the cohort factor is a nuisance, not a ranked
construct. Sampling uses a raised `target_accept` for the global-local funnel.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_horseshoe.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
